report_markdown:
# 2026 FIFA World Cup — Full Knockout-Stage Prediction Report

## Executive Summary

Using the Elo-Poisson baseline (μ=1.4) blended ~30% with the DraftKings 1X2 prior and ~70% with contextual reasoning (form, H2H, host advantage, tactical matchups), I project **Argentina vs France in the Final at MetLife Stadium**, with **Argentina defending the trophy at 54% title probability**. Brazil, Spain, and England form the second tier; Germany and Portugal sit at the dark-horse boundary. The bracket remains fluid in the bottom half (Spain/Argentina/Portugal all clustered near λ≈1.0), and the single highest-leverage upset risk is **Ecuador eliminating France in the Round of 32**. Simulation social-media channels were silent across all five rounds (0 posts, 0 interviews), so all probability adjustments below are model-driven.

---

## 1. Group Stage — Final Standings & MD2/MD3 Match Predictions

Standings predicted from combined Elo-Poisson + bookmaker calibration. Per-match: team_a_win / draw / team_b_win, most_likely_score, top_3_scores.

### Group A (Mexico City)
| Pos | Team | Pts |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Mexico | 7 |
| 2 | South Korea | 5 |
| 3 | Czech Republic | 4 |
| 4 | South Africa | 0 |

**MD2 — South Korea vs Czech Republic** (6/19 implied): SK 39 / D 38 / CZE 23 · MLS 0-0 · top3: 0-0 (24.7%), 1-0 SK (20.5%), 0-1 CZE (14.1%)
**MD2 — Mexico vs South Africa**: MEX 55 / D 34 / RSA 11 · MLS 1-0 · top3: 1-0 (26.6%), 0-0 (24.7%), 2-0 (14.3%)
**MD3 — Mexico vs South Korea** (6/24 19:00 fix): MEX 41 / D 36 / SK 23 · MLS 1-0 · top3: 1-0 (22.0%), 0-0 (24.7%), 2-0 (10.8%)
